Package domain

Class SensorNode

java.lang.Object
domain.SensorNode
public final class SensorNode extends Object
Represents a wireless sensor node in the UAV data-collection network.

Immutable value object. Position updates return a new instance via withPosition(double, double), preserving the original. Equality and hashing are based solely on id, so two nodes with the same identifier compare equal regardless of their current coordinates (a node retains its identity as it moves through the environment).

Velocity snapshot

When a sensor node is observed directly by the UAV, the snapshot also captures the node's movement direction (0–7, same encoding as NodeMover) and speed (cells per tick) at the time of observation. This enables the UAV to compute a predicted intercept position rather than chasing the node's last-known static location. Nodes discovered only via transitive neighbour-cache transfer use the no-velocity constructor and return false from hasVelocity().

This class belongs to the domain layer and is shared by both the algorithm layer (path planning) and the simulation layer (environment).

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SensorNode(int id, double x, double y)
    Constructs a sensor node with position only (no velocity information).
    SensorNode(int id, double x, double y, int direction, int speed)
    Constructs a sensor node with full velocity information.

  • Constructor Details

    • SensorNode

      public SensorNode(int id, double x, double y)
      Constructs a sensor node with position only (no velocity information).

      Use this constructor for algorithm-layer objects and tests where movement direction is not relevant. hasVelocity() will return false on the resulting instance.

      Parameters:
      id - unique node identifier (typically assigned sequentially from 5000)
      x - x-coordinate in the environment grid
      y - y-coordinate in the environment grid

    • SensorNode

      public SensorNode(int id, double x, double y, int direction, int speed)
      Constructs a sensor node with full velocity information.

      Used by NodeAgent.toSensorNode() to capture a complete state snapshot for interception-aware path following.

      Parameters:
      id - unique node identifier
      x - x-coordinate in the environment grid
      y - y-coordinate in the environment grid
      direction - movement direction at observation time (0–7); use -1 if unknown
      speed - movement speed at observation time (cells per tick); use 0 if unknown

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public int getId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this node.
      Returns:
      node ID

    • getX

      public double getX()
      Returns the current x-coordinate of this node.
      Returns:
      x-coordinate

    • getY

      public double getY()
      Returns the current y-coordinate of this node.
      Returns:
      y-coordinate

    • getDirection

      public int getDirection()
      Returns the movement direction at the time of observation (0–7, same encoding as NodeMover). Returns -1 if no velocity information was captured.
      Returns:
      direction (0–7) or -1 if unknown

    • getSpeed

      public int getSpeed()
      Returns the movement speed (cells per tick) at the time of observation. Returns 0 if no velocity information was captured.
      Returns:
      speed (cells per tick) or 0 if unknown

    • hasVelocity

      public boolean hasVelocity()
      Returns true if this snapshot carries valid velocity information (direction and speed were recorded at observation time).

      When false, the UAV falls back to targeting the last-known static position rather than computing a predicted intercept.

      Returns:
      true if direction ≥ 0 and speed > 0

    • calculateDistance

      public double calculateDistance(SensorNode other)
      Computes the Euclidean distance between this node and another. 
        distance = √((x₂−x₁)² + (y₂−y₁)²)
      Parameters:
      other - the target node; must not be null
      Returns:
      Euclidean distance (≥ 0); returns 0 when both nodes occupy the same position
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if other is null

    • withPosition

      public SensorNode withPosition(double newX, double newY)
      Returns a new SensorNode with the same ID and velocity but updated coordinates. The original instance is unchanged (immutable update pattern).
      Parameters:
      newX - updated x-coordinate
      newY - updated y-coordinate
      Returns:
      new instance with the same ID, same velocity, and updated position

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Two sensor nodes are equal if and only if their IDs are equal, regardless of current position.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
